title: OpenAI Increases Infrastructure Budget to $750 Billion, First $20 Billion Invested in Georgia Data Center
excerpt: On July 22, 2026, OpenAI announced that it plans to invest $75 billion in AI infrastructure before 2030, an increase of about 25% compared to earlier estimates
content_html:
On July 22, 2026, OpenAI announced that it plans to invest $75 billion in AI infrastructure before 2030, an increase of about 25% compared to earlier estimates this year. This move marks OpenAI's renewed focus on long-term computing power advantages through more aggressive capital expenditures, amid the stagnation of its flagship data center project "Stargate."

As the first project under this large investment plan, OpenAI intends to build a 1,400-acre "Project Camellia" data center campus in the northwest of Savannah, Georgia, with a cost of $2 billion. The campus has partnered with Georgia Power and is expected to obtain at least 3.2 gigawatts of electricity supply between 2028 and 2032, with all new infrastructure and power service costs borne by OpenAI. To reduce the impact on the local power grid, OpenAI has committed to reducing peak load by up to 1 gigawatt, and the project has received a 15-year 50% property tax exemption from Effingham County.

Despite the massive power consumption, neither party disclosed specific details about the transition to clean energy. Regulatory documents show that Georgia Power plans to add nearly 9.9 gigawatts of power generation capacity, with over half (about 5.8 gigawatts) relying on natural gas, while the rest will be supplemented by photovoltaic and energy storage systems, raising concerns in the market about the increased reliance on fossil fuels and carbon emissions due to computing power expansion. Notably, OpenAI recently hired Brett Mayo, who previously led the construction of xAI's Memphis Colossus data center, to oversee its data center operations, possibly indicating that the first GPU deployment nodes may be established before 2028.

At this critical stage of generative AI entering advanced model training and large-scale inference applications, the implementation of OpenAI's substantial infrastructure funding highlights not only the deep competition among leading manufacturers for computing power and electricity resources, but also the real challenges faced by ultra-large-scale AI data center construction in reshaping regional power grid structures and promoting energy structure transformation.

sentences.en: Notably, OpenAI recently hired Brett Mayo, who previously led the construction of xAI's Memphis Colossus data center, to oversee its data center operations, possibly indicating that the first GPU deployment nodes may be established before 2028.
sentences.zh: 值得注意的是，OpenAI 最近聘请了曾领导 xAI 孟菲斯巨像数据中心建设的 Brett Mayo 来监督其数据中心运营，这可能表明第一个 GPU 部署节点可能会在 2028 年之前建立。

blocks.html_en: At this critical stage of generative AI entering advanced model training and large-scale inference applications, the implementation of OpenAI's substantial infrastructure funding highlights not only the deep competition among leading manufacturers for computing power and electricity resources, but also the real challenges faced by ultra-large-scale AI data center construction in reshaping regional power grid structures and promoting energy structure transformation.

sentences.en: At this critical stage of generative AI entering advanced model training and large-scale inference applications, the implementation of OpenAI's substantial infrastructure funding highlights not only the deep competition among leading manufacturers for computing power and electricity resources, but also the real challenges faced by ultra-large-scale AI data center construction in reshaping regional power grid structures and promoting energy structure transformation.
sentences.zh: 在生成式人工智能进入高级模型训练和大规模推理应用的关键阶段，OpenAI巨额基础设施资金的落地，不仅凸显了领先厂商之间对算力和电力资源的深度竞争，也凸显了超大规模人工智能数据中心建设在重塑地区电网结构、推动能源结构转型方面面临的现实挑战。

content_html_zh:
2026年7月22日，OpenAI宣布计划在2030年之前投资750亿美元用于人工智能基础设施，比今年早些时候的估计增长约25%。 此举标志着OpenAI在其旗舰数据中心项目“Stargate”陷入停滞的情况下，通过更积极的资本支出重新关注长期算力优势。

作为这一大型投资计划下的第一个项目，OpenAI拟在佐治亚州萨凡纳西北部建设占地1400英亩的“Project Camellia”数据中心园区，耗资20亿美元。 该园区已与佐治亚电力公司合作，预计在2028年至2032年间获得至少3.2吉瓦的电力供应，所有新建基础设施和电力服务费用均由OpenAI承担。 为了减少对当地电网的影响，OpenAI 承诺将峰值负荷减少最多 1 吉瓦，该项目还获得了埃芬汉县 15 年 50% 的财产税豁免。

尽管用电量巨大，但双方都没有透露向清洁能源转型的具体细节。 监管文件显示，佐治亚电力计划新增近9.9吉瓦发电容量，其中一半以上（约5.8吉瓦）依赖天然气，其余将通过光伏和储能系统补充，引发市场对算力扩张导致对化石燃料依赖增加和碳排放增加的担忧。 值得注意的是，OpenAI 最近聘请了曾领导 xAI 孟菲斯巨像数据中心建设的 Brett Mayo 来监督其数据中心运营，这可能表明第一个 GPU 部署节点可能会在 2028 年之前建立。

在生成式人工智能进入高级模型训练和大规模推理应用的关键阶段，OpenAI巨额基础设施资金的落地，不仅凸显了领先厂商之间对算力和电力资源的深度竞争，也凸显了超大规模人工智能数据中心建设在重塑地区电网结构、推动能源结构转型方面面临的现实挑战。
